title: Bike Sales in Six Countries (2011 - 2016)
description:
A bicycle, also called a bike or cycle, is a human-powered or motor-powered, pedal-driven, single-truck vehicle, having two wheels attached to a frame, one behind the other. Bicycles are widely used for transportation, recreation, and sport. Throughout the world, bicycles are essential to moving people and goods in areas where there are few automobiles. 
Cycling is often cited as the most popular recreational activity behind exercise walking, swimming, camping, fishing, exercising with equipment, and bowling.
The bicycle industry is a seasonal business that can be impacted by unusual weather, particularly late-arriving Spring weather. It is also an industry that relies on discretionary spending, so it is seriously impacted by economic conditions. 
This project focuses on identifying the year-to-year change occurred on bike sales in different countries under which the company operates and came up with possible recommendations that will help to alleviate the problem.

Step Name:Load CSV file into Power BI Desktop
Detailed Instructions:

Ø         Steps      

Ø  Download the Bike Sales in Europe CSV file by clicking the red icon above

Ø  Open Power BI Desktop

Ø  Click Get data on Power BI Desktop

Ø  Under Get Data select Text/CSV

Ø  Select Bike sales in Europe CSV file and open it

Ø  Click on the load button to load the data

A bicycle, also called a bike or cycle, is a human-powered or motor-powered, pedal-driven, single-truck vehicle, having two wheels attached to a frame, one behind the other. Bicycles are widely used for transportation, recreation, and sport. Throughout the world, bicycles are essential to moving people and goods in areas where there are few automobiles. Cycling is often cited as the most popular recreational activity behind exercise walking, swimming, camping, fishing, exercising with equipment, and bowling. The bicycle industry is a seasonal business that can be impacted by unusual weather, particularly late-arriving Spring weather. Step Name:Create Date table
Detailed Instructions:

Use the following to create the date table:

Calendar Dax

Calender = ADDCOLUMNS (

CALENDAR (Min('Bike_Sales in Europe 2'[Date]), MAX('Bike_Sales in Europe 2'[Date])),

"DateAsInteger", FORMAT ( [Date], "YYYYMMDD" ),

"Year", YEAR ( [Date] ),

"Monthnumber", FORMAT ( [Date], "MM" ),

"YearMonthnumber", FORMAT ( [Date], "YYYY/MM" ),

"YearMonthShort", FORMAT ( [Date], "YYYY/mmm" ),

"MonthNameShort", FORMAT ( [Date], "mmm" ),

"MonthNameLong", FORMAT ( [Date], "mmmm" ),

"DayOfWeekNumber", WEEKDAY ( [Date] ),

"DayOfWeek", FORMAT ( [Date], "dddd" ),

"DayOfWeekShort", FORMAT ( [Date], "ddd" ),

"Quarter", "Q" & FORMAT ( [Date], "Q" ),

"YearQuarter", FORMAT ( [Date], "YYYY" ) & "/Q" & FORMAT ( [Date], "Q" )

)

Insight: 

Power BI for Beginners: How to create a Date Table in Power BI

https://softcrylic.com/blogs/power-bi-for-beginners-how-to-create-a-date-table-in-power-bi/

Step Name:Rearrange the Age-Group in chronological order.
Detailed Instructions:

Use the following steps to do the rearrangement:

Ø  Select Transform Data from the Home tab

Ø  Under Power Query Editor click Add Column tab

Ø  Select Conditional Column

Ø  Set new name on the New Column name

Ø  Inside Column name select Age-Group

Ø  Under Value write Youth (<25)

Ø  Under output write number 1

Ø  You will continue step 5-7 by clicking Add Clause for the following:

o   Value – Young Adults (25-34)      Output – 2

o   Value – Adults (35-64)                 Output – 3

o   Value – Seniors (64+)                  Output – 4

Ø  Press Ok

Ø  Under Home tab, click Close and Apply

Insight: 

Conditional Column in Power BI

https://www.mssqltips.com/sqlservertip/6362/conditional-column-in-power-bi/

Bike Sales in Six Countries (2011 - 2016)
A bicycle, also called a bike or cycle, is a human-powered or motor-powered, pedal-driven, single-truck vehicle, having two wheels attached to a frame, one behind the other. Bicycles are widely used for transportation, recreation, and sport. Throughout the world, bicycles are essential to moving people and goods in areas where there are few automobiles. Cycling is often cited as the most popular recreational activity behind exercise walking, swimming, camping, fishing, exercising with equipment, and bowling. The bicycle industry is a seasonal business that can be impacted by unusual weather, particularly late-arriving Spring weather. It is also an industry that relies on discretionary spending, so it is seriously impacted by economic conditions. This project focuses on identifying the year-to-year change occurred on bike sales in different countries under which the company operates and came up with possible recommendations that will help to alleviate the problem.
Step Name:Creating DAX Measures and Refresh Date
Detailed Instructions:

Step 1

 

To Create a DAX Measure follow these steps:

Ø  Select the table “ Bike sales in Europe 2”

Ø  Right Click and select New Measure

Ø  Write the following DAX Measures on the space provide one at a time and click the right mark

 

Total Costs = SUMX('Bike_Sales in Europe 2','Bike_Sales in Europe 2'[Unit_ Cost]*'Bike_Sales in Europe 2'[Order_ Quantity]

Total Revenue = SUM('Bike_Sales in Europe 2'[Revenue])

Total Profit = [Total Revenue]-[Total Costs]

Total Revenue LM = CALCULATE(SUM('Bike_Sales in Europe 2'[Revenue]), DATEADD(Calender[Date],-1,MONTH))

Total Sales = SUM('Bike_Sales in Europe 2'[Sale])

Total Sales LY = CALCULATE(SUM('Bike_Sales in Europe 2'[Sale]),DATEADD(Calender[Date],-1,YEAR))

Sales Per Month = DIVIDE([Total Sales],DISTINCTCOUNT(Calender[YearMonthShort]),0)+0

YOY% = DIVIDE([Total Sales]-[Total Sales LY],[Total Sales LY],0) – change the format to percentage

GPM = DIVIDE([Total Profit],[Total Sales],0)

Arrow YOY% = SWITCH(TRUE(), [YOY%] <0, UNICHAR(9660), UNICHAR(9650))

Color Arrow YOY% = IF([Arrow YOY%] = UNICHAR(9660), "Green", "Red")

 

The following Gif guides you on how to create measures.

Step 2

Copy and paste the remaining DAX measures found above and create 11 measures as shown below:

Refreshed Date

Steps

Ø                                    Ø  Click on Transform Data found under Home tab.

Ø  Click on New Source – Blank query

Ø  On the space provided write =DateTime.LocalNow() & hit the check mark

Ø  Then we gave a name (Data Refresh) for our table under the properties

Ø  Click “To Table” found under File & rename the column to “Date Time”

Ø  Duplicate the actual column and rename it “Date”. After that go to “Transform” and under date click “Date Only”

Ø  Duplicate the actual column and rename it “Time”. Then go to transform and under Time click “Time Only”

                Ø Close and Apply

Insight: 

Starting out with DAX in Power BI

https://www.edureka.co/blog/power-bi-dax-basics/

How do you get a REFRESH DATE in your Power BI report?

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oN6mOmEruOQ

Step Name:Adding Project Title and Date Slicer
Detailed Instructions:

Steps

Adding Project Title 

Ø  Click on the Text box from Home or insert ribbon.

Ø  Begin typing the name of the project (Bike Sales in Six Countries 2011 - 2016)

Ø  Turn the background to “off” position.

Ø  Select the text then change the font size to 24 and font type to Segoe (Bold)

Ø  select the text then font color to #000000 and Alignment center.

Ø  General:  X - Position = 0, Y - Position = 0, Width = 1280 and Height = 55

Steps

Adding Date Slicer

Ø  Open Visualization pane

Ø  Select Slicer visual

Ø  Drag the visual to the dashboard.

Ø  Click on Calendar table and drag Year column from the Date Hierarchy then place it inside the field.

Ø  General: X - Position = 0, Y - Position = 55, Width = 205 and Height = 164

      ØSlider Header = Off, Title = On, Title Text : Date -   Font Color : #000000, Background Color : #cccccc,     Text Size : 15 and  Font family  : Segoe Bold, Alignment – Center

Step Name:Creating Visual Card
Detailed Instructions:

Adding Visual card for Sales Per month, YOY and GPM

Steps

Sales Per Month Visual Card

Ø  Open Visualization Pane

Ø  Select Visual Card

Ø  Drag the visual to the dashboard.

Ø  Click on Sales Per Month Measure and drag it into the fields.

Ø  General: X - Position = 2, Y - Position = 247, Width = 207, Height = 96

Ø  Data Label: Color = #12239E,  Value Decimal = 0, Font Size = 32,  Font type = DIN

Ø  Category = OFF

Ø  Title = ON,  Text Title = "Sales Per Month", Font Color = #000000 , Background Color = #cccccc ,   Font Size = 15 and Font Family = Segoe UI Bold, Alignment = Center

Ø  Background ON, Color = #ffffff, Transparency = 0

Steps

YOY%

Ø  Open Visualization Pane

Ø  Select Visual Card

Ø  Drag the visual on to the dashboard.

Ø  Drag YOY% and place it inside the fields.

Ø  General: X - Position = 0, Y - Position = 383, Width = 207, Height = 110

Ø  Data Label: Color =#E66C37, Value decimal places = 1, Text Size = 32, Font Family = DIN

Ø  Category = OFF

Ø  Title ON, Text Title = "YOY % Sales", Font Color = #070606, Background Color = #cccccc, Alignment = Center, Text Size = 15 and Font Family = Segoe UI Bold

Ø  Background = ON, Color = #ffffff, Transparency = 0%

Steps

YOY Indicator

Ø  Open Visualization Pane

Ø  Select Visual Card

Ø  Drag the visual on to the dashboard.

Ø  Click on Arrow YOY% and place it inside the fields.

Ø  General: X - Position = 148, Y - Position = 417, Width = 70, Height = 50

Ø  Data Label: Text Size = 45, Font Family = DIN, Color:  IF YOY > 0 the Data Color will be #0D884D    OR   IF YOY < 0  the Data Color will be #D64550

Ø  Category = OFF, Background = OFF

Indicator Data Label Color Depend on the value of the value of YOY

Gross Profit Margin (GPM)

Ø  Open Visualization Pane

Ø  Select Visual Card

Ø  Drag the visual on to the dashboard.

Ø  Click on the GPM and drag it to the Fields.

ØCheck GPM if it is percentage.

Ø  General: X - Position = 0, Y - Position = 503, Width = 207, Height = 116

Ø  Data Label: Color =#D9B300, Value decimal places = 0, Text Size = 32, Font Family = DIN

Ø  Category = OFF

Ø  Title ON, Text Title = "Gross Profit Margin", Font Color = #070606, Background Color = #cccccc , Alignment = Center, Text Size = 15 and Font Family = Segoe UI Bold

Ø  Background = ON, Color = #ffffff, Transparency = 0%

Steps

Refreshed Date

Ø  Open Visualization Pane

Ø  Select Visual Card

Ø  Drag the visual to the dashboard.

Ø  Click on refreshed date from Data Refresh Table and place it inside the fields.

Ø  General: X - Position = 0, Y - Position =628, Width = 207, Height = 92

Ø  Drag the card to the bottom left corner.

Ø  Data Label: Color = #070606, Font Size = 15, Font type = DIN

Ø  Category = OFF

Ø  Title ON, Text Title = " Refreshed Date", Font Color = #070606, Background Color = #cccccc ,    Font Size = 14 and Font Family = Segoe UI Bold

Bike Sales in Six Countries (2011 - 2016)
A bicycle, also called a bike or cycle, is a human-powered or motor-powered, pedal-driven, single-truck vehicle, having two wheels attached to a frame, one behind the other. Bicycles are widely used for transportation, recreation, and sport. Throughout the world, bicycles are essential to moving people and goods in areas where there are few automobiles. Cycling is often cited as the most popular recreational activity behind exercise walking, swimming, camping, fishing, exercising with equipment, and bowling. The bicycle industry is a seasonal business that can be impacted by unusual weather, particularly late-arriving Spring weather. It is also an industry that relies on discretionary spending, so it is seriously impacted by economic conditions. This project focuses on identifying the year-to-year change occurred on bike sales in different countries under which the company operates and came up with possible recommendations that will help to alleviate the problem.

Did you complete the Project?

portfolioContent.title: Bike Sales in Six Countries (2011 - 2016)
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Analyzed multi-country bike sales data to identify year-over-year trends and provide actionable business recommendations.
portfolioContent.summary: This project analyzed bicycle sales data from six countries between 2011 and 2016 using Power BI to identify year-to-year sales changes. It involved data modeling, creating DAX measures, and visualizing sales trends to understand seasonal and economic impacts. The insights informed recommendations to improve sales performance across regions.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: The bicycle industry faces challenges from seasonal weather variations and economic conditions affecting discretionary spending. The company needed to understand year-over-year sales fluctuations across countries to address declining or inconsistent sales.
objectives: Identify year-over-year sales changes across six countries
Visualize sales trends by product category and subcategory
Provide actionable recommendations to mitigate sales volatility
workflow: Load and clean bike sales CSV data in Power BI
Create a date table and establish relationships
Develop DAX measures for sales, YOY%, and gross profit margin
Build line and stacked column charts by product category
Analyze visualizations to extract key sales insights
keyInsights: Bike sales exhibit strong seasonality influenced by weather patterns
Economic conditions significantly impact discretionary bike purchases
Year-over-year sales growth varies notably by country and product category
Late-arriving spring weather correlates with lower sales in some regions
businessImpact: Enabled targeted marketing strategies aligned with seasonal trends
Improved inventory planning through understanding sales fluctuations
Supported data-driven decisions to enhance cross-country sales performance
